compressed_view: Cross-skill UX Foundations application playbook for 13 principles (Fitts's Law, Hick's Law, Visual Hierarchy, Data Viz, Feedback, Error Recovery, Dark Mode, Motion Safety, IA, Interaction, Permission, State, Accessibility, Motion, Content, Platform). /design maps principles to tokens: Fitts's Law to AppSize.ctaHeight, Hick's Law to single Brand.primary accent, Visual Hierarchy to Text.primary/secondary/tertiary, Data Viz to Chart.* tokens, Motion Safety to AppSpring/AppEasing/motionSafe. /ux produces ux-research.md (principle mapping) + ux-spec.md (9 sections) + compliance gateway (5/5) + heuristic validation (12/13). /qa verifies: button frames >=44pt, all 5 states tested, VoiceOver labels + Dynamic Type AX5, reduceMotion respected, event names match taxonomy. /dev builds bottom-up: tokens T1-T3, components T4-T5, services T6, analytics T7, assembly T8, a11y T11, pbxproj T12. Principle gaps requiring new tokens are DS evolution items proposed on feature branches.

task_signature.context: Cross-skill playbook for applying UX Foundations to any screen. Used by /design (token mapping), /ux (spec authoring), /qa (accessibility testing), /dev (implementation checklist). Single source of truth for the 13 principles.

learned_patterns.pattern: All 13 UX Foundations principles — quick reference
learned_patterns.decision: ux-foundations.md is organized in 10 Parts. The 13 measurable principles are: (1.1) Fitts's Law, (1.2) Hick's Law, (1.3) Visual Hierarchy / Signal-to-Noise, (1.4) Data Visualization Clarity, (1.5) Feedback & System Status, (1.6) Error Recovery, (1.7) Dark Mode & Adaptive Color, (1.8) Motion Safety, (2.x) Information Architecture, (3.x) Interaction Patterns, (4.x) Data Viz Patterns, (5.x) Permission & Trust, (6.x) State Patterns, (7.x) Accessibility Standards, (8.x) Micro-Interactions & Motion, (9.x) Content Strategy, (10.x) Platform-Specific Patterns. Home v2 mapped 12/13 (1 N/A). Training v2 applied all.
source_executions: home-today-screen state.json ux_or_integration note: 'ux-research.md (12/13 principles mapped)'

learned_patterns.decision: /design uses principles to select tokens: Fitts's Law → AppSize.ctaHeight (52pt) + frame(maxWidth:.infinity). Hick's Law → AppColor.Brand.primary as sole accent. Visual Hierarchy → AppColor.Text.primary/.secondary/.tertiary layering. Data Viz → AppColor.Chart.* tokens. Motion Safety → AppSpring, AppEasing, motionSafe tokens (added Home v2 T2).

1.7 Dark Mode: All AppColor.* adaptive tokens — never raw hex

source_executions: home-today-screen v2 T1-T3 (token additions tied to principle gaps)

learned_patterns.decision: /ux produces: (1) ux-research.md — maps each principle to the specific surface, (2) ux-spec.md — 9 sections including Principle Application Table, (3) compliance gateway check (5 dimensions), (4) heuristic validation (12/13 pass). The Principle Application Table in ux-spec.md Section 9 is the traceability matrix between principles and spec decisions.
learned_patterns.principle_application_table_format: | Principle | Status | How honored in this feature |
source_executions: home-today-screen state.json ux_or_integration: '12/13 principles mapped', 'heuristic validation 12/12 pass'

learned_patterns.decision: /qa verifies: (1) Fitts's Law — accessibilityFrame ≥ 44pt on all interactive elements, (2) State Patterns (6.x) — all states (empty/loading/error/partial/full) covered in tests, (3) Accessibility (7.x) — VoiceOver labels verified via XCUITest, Dynamic Type at AX5, (4) Motion Safety (1.8) — animations respect reduceMotion setting, (5) Analytics (H section of checklist) — event names match taxonomy CSV.

qa_checks_by_principle.1.1 Fitts's Law: XCUITest: assert button frame height >= 44, assert CTA frame maxWidth == screen width
qa_checks_by_principle.6.x State Patterns: XCTest: test all 5 states (empty, loading, error, partial, full data)
qa_checks_by_principle.7.x Accessibility: XCUITest: accessibilityLabel not nil for all interactive elements, Dynamic Type AX5
qa_checks_by_principle.1.8 Motion Safety: XCTest: verify animations wrap in withAnimation only when !accessibilityReduceMotion
qa_checks_by_principle.H Analytics: XCTest: MockAnalyticsService captures correct event name + params

learned_patterns.decision: /dev uses foundations as implementation checklist in Phase 4. Bottom-up build order: tokens first (T1-T3) → components (T4-T5) → services (T6) → analytics enums (T7) → main view assembly (T8) → a11y pass (T11) → pbxproj swap (T12). Do NOT write view code before all token/component dependencies are added to AppTheme.swift and AppComponents.swift.

implementation_checklist_by_principle.1.1 Fitts's Law: frame(maxWidth: .infinity).frame(height: AppSize.ctaHeight) on all primary CTAs
implementation_checklist_by_principle.1.2 Hick's Law: Single primary action surfaced per screen section. Progressive disclosure for advanced options.
implementation_checklist_by_principle.1.3 Visual Hierarchy: AppColor.Text.* layering: primary → secondary → tertiary, never raw Color.*
implementation_checklist_by_principle.1.5 Feedback: AppColor.Status.* for all feedback states. Empty state view for no-data condition.
implementation_checklist_by_principle.1.7 Dark Mode: Zero raw hex or Color(red:green:blue:) in any view. All AppColor.* tokens are adaptive.
implementation_checklist_by_principle.6.x States: Explicit ViewState enum: .loading, .empty, .error(String), .loaded(Data). Each state rendered by dedicated sub-view or conditional branch.

learned_patterns.pattern: Principle gaps that require DS Evolution (new tokens/components)
learned_patterns.decision: When a principle is violated in v1 AND no token exists to fix it, the fix requires DS evolution: propose new token on feature branch, add to AppTheme.swift, run tokens-check. Home v2 DS evolution: AppText/AppSize tokens (T1), motion tokens AppSpring/AppEasing (T2), AppColor.Chart.weight/hrv/heartRate (T3). Training v2 DS evolution: AppSize.tabBarClearance, AppText.monoCaption. New tokens always proposed on feature branch, never directly on main.
source_executions: home-today-screen T1, T2, T3 — DS evolution tasks
training-plan-v2 state.json: '2 new tokens (AppSize.tabBarClearance, AppText.monoCaption), 3 shared components promoted'

anti_patterns.pattern: Applying foundations only in /ux phase and forgetting in /dev phase
anti_patterns.what_went_wrong: Phase 3 compliance gateway passes but Phase 4 implementation re-introduces raw literals or skips state coverage. Each skill has its own checklist against foundations. The v2-refactor-checklist Sections C-K are the /dev+/qa verification that foundations were actually implemented.
anti_patterns.source_execution: v2-refactor-checklist.md Sections B-K — cover the full implementation stack

anti_patterns.pattern: Mapping 0 principles as N/A without justification
anti_patterns.what_went_wrong: Every principle must be Pass OR N/A WITH justification. 'N/A' without rationale is a shortcut. Home v2 had exactly 1 N/A — fully documented. If you're marking more than 2 principles N/A, the spec is probably incomplete.
anti_patterns.source_execution: home-today-screen ux_or_integration note: '12/13 principles mapped, 1 N/A'

speedup_instructions: This is the shared reference for all skills during any UI feature work. /ux reads the principle list and spec format. /design reads the principle_to_token mapping. /dev reads the implementation_checklist_by_principle. /qa reads the qa_checks_by_principle. Cross-reference with ux-spec-patterns.json (L1 ux cache) for the full 9-section spec structure and with ux-foundations-application.json (L1 design cache) for the token-level details.
